caseStudy.title: Sales Process Automation
caseStudy.description: Eliminating manual handoffs across the sales cycle.
caseStudy.overview: A high-growth SaaS company's sales process was bottlenecked by manual handoffs between marketing, SDRs, account executives, and customer success teams. Deals slipped through the cracks during transitions, and leadership lacked real-time pipeline visibility.
caseStudy.problem: Lead routing was manual and inconsistent, with new inbound leads sometimes waiting over 24 hours for assignment. Contract generation required copying data between systems, introducing errors. CRM updates depended on rep discipline rather than automation.
caseStudy.solution: An automated lead routing engine was built with territory-based assignment and round-robin distribution. Contract generation was automated by pulling deal data directly from the CRM into pre-approved templates. CRM activities were auto-logged from email and calendar integrations, removing the manual update burden from reps.

results.summary: The automated sales process has become a key driver of the company's revenue growth. Leadership now has real-time pipeline visibility, enabling more accurate forecasting and faster course correction.
